name: Slack
mcp_notes: Slack has released an official MCP server to allow AI agents to securely access Slack workspace data.
api_notes: The Slack platform offers a comprehensive Web API and Events API. It is highly broad, allowing for the creation of bots, workflows, and integrations that can manipulate most objects within Slack.

evidence.claim: Slack provides a Web API for building apps, agents, and integrations.
Slack offers an official Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.
Developers can create apps for free by signing in to their Slack account.
Search citation: How to properly interact with Slack’s API — The Slack Way | by Hugo Lu | Medium

access_notes: A developer can create a free account and register apps for development purposes via the Slack API dashboard. Access to specific paid features or Enterprise features may require higher-tier plans.
one_liner: Slack is a messaging and collaboration platform that provides an extensive API for building custom apps, agents, and automated workflows.
auth_notes: Slack uses OAuth2 for app authorization and typically issues Bearer tokens (Bot User OAuth Tokens) to authenticate API requests.
blocker: Commercial distribution of apps typically requires agreement to Slack's Marketplace terms or a separate partnership/agreement.
